basicDetails.implementingAgency: Secondary Education Department, Government Of Uttarakhand

schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to identify and support the brightest Class 12 Science stream students by providing them with high-quality coaching and guidance for engineering and medical entrance examinations. The programme is a unique state-level initiative approved by the Government of India.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The scheme “Super-100 Programme” is implemented under the Samagra Shiksha Programme by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to identify and support the brightest Class 12 Science stream students by providing them with high-quality coaching and guidance for engineering and medical entrance examinations. The programme is a unique state-level initiative approved by the Government of India.
schemeContent.benefits_md: Under this programme, 100 Science students were prepared for engineering and medical entrance examinations in 2023–24. Faculty members were selected from reputed coaching institutes based on their interviews and experience.

children.text: The scheme “Super-100 Programme” is implemented under the Samagra Shiksha Programme by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to identify and support the brightest Class 12 Science stream students by providing them with high-quality coaching and guidance for engineering and medical entrance examinations. The programme is a unique state-level initiative approved by the Government of India.

Under this programme, 100 Science students were prepared for engineering and medical entrance examinations in 2023–24. Faculty members were selected from reputed coaching institutes based on their interviews and experience.
From each block, Class 12 Science stream students were selected who had secured the first position in Class 10 within their respective development block.

applicationProcess.process_md: From each block, Class 12 Science stream students were selected who had secured the first position in Class 10 within their respective development block.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The applicant must be a Class 12 Science stream student studying in Uttarakhand. 1. One top-performing student from each block, along with the highest-scoring students from the SC, ST, and OBC categories, will be considered eligible under this programme. 1. A total of 100 Class 12 Science stream students will be selected for the programme.

children.text: The applicant must be a Class 12 Science stream student studying in Uttarakhand.
One top-performing student from each block, along with the highest-scoring students from the SC, ST, and OBC categories, will be considered eligible under this programme.
A total of 100 Class 12 Science stream students will be selected for the programme.
